evaluation.test_case: Phase 11 commit message format for coding-task-workflow

non_negotiable_rule.text: Phase 11 commit messages use a conventional-commits subject, a body that references the work-item slug and parent issue, then a blank-line-separated trailer block. For GitHub Copilot CLI, include Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>.

steps: Include body linking to work-item slug and parent issue
Append Co-authored-by trailer separated by blank line

subject_line_rules.subject: imperative_present_tense_lowercase_no_period
examples: feat(retry): add exponential backoff with jitter to HTTP client
fix(csv-import): prevent silent row drops during parsing
refactor(auth): migrate password hashing to Argon2id

example_commit_message.subject: feat(http-client): add exponential backoff with jitter to retry mechanism
example_commit_message.body:
This work implements a configurable retry mechanism for the HTTP client with
exponential backoff and jitter. Retries are capped at 5 attempts and only occur
for status codes 429, 500, 502, 503, and 504. All code paths are covered by TDD
test cases written before implementation.

Work item: 2026-04-23-add-retry-mechanism
Closes #42
example_commit_message.structure_notes: Subject on line 1, blank line 2, body lines 3-N, blank line, trailer at end

validation_checklist: Subject line uses conventional commits format <type>(<scope>): <subject>
Body references parent issue (e.g., #42 or Closes #42)
No trailing whitespace or blank lines after trailer
why_this_format_matters: Auditability: GitHub issues, PRs, and git log remain synchronized
Resumability: work-item slug links back to GitHub issue hierarchy
Tool compatibility: conventional commits parsed by CI, release notes, changelog tools
Traceability: parent issue appears in commit for git grep searches

evaluation_result.finding: Phase 11 requires a strict conventional-commits format with three sections: (1) conventional-commits subject line, (2) body with work-item slug and parent issue reference, (3) Co-authored-by trailer separated by blank lines. For GitHub Copilot CLI, the trailer must use the official Copilot email.
evaluation_result.rationale: The specification is explicit in SKILL.md non-negotiable rule #5 and references/workflow.md Phase 11 section. The format supports auditability, resumability, and tool compatibility across the entire workflow.
